title: Cyclosorus matutumensis
taxonomy: Cyclosorus matutumensis belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The genus Cyclosorus is a part of the order Polypodiales. It is a species of fern, a vascular plant that belongs to the division Pteridophyta. The classification of Cyclosorus matutumensis is further divided into kingdom Plantae. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships between different plant species.
uses: Cyclosorus matutumensis, also known as the Matutum fern, is a plant species that has been traditionally used in some cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin infections. In some regions, it is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other health conditions. Additionally, the plant's leaves are also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Cyclosorus matutumensis.

intro: Cyclosorus matutumensis, also known as the Matutum fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus matutumensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
